name: Haywood Henry
abstract: Frank Haywood Henry (January 10, 1913 – September 15, 1994) was an American jazz baritone saxophonist. In 1978 he was inducted into the Alabama Jazz Hall of Fame.

concerts.label: Newport Jazz Festival: Salute to Ella Fitzgerald
The New York Jazz Repertory Company: Duke Ellington 75th Birthday Salute

about_quotes.speaker: Erskine Hawkins
about_quotes.evidence: Haywood Henry was in this band... 'Bama State Collegians was the first one... Haywood joined us. That made the fourth sax.

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Barefield
about_quotes.evidence: they had about ten of us, Joe Bill Newman, Norris Turner and me and Heywood Henry and a bunch of us went out there and was teaching underprivileged children out there

about_quotes.speaker: Tiny Grimes
about_quotes.evidence: Heywood Henry was on saxophone on one of the numbers... I think he musta played baritone, I think.

about_quotes.speaker: Phil Schaap
about_quotes.evidence: Heywood Henry is on “I Put A Spell On You”... Heywood Henry of the Erskine Hawkins band
about_quotes.doc_title: Phil Schaap interviewed by Monk Rowe, Toronto, Canada, January 9, 2003

about_quotes.evidence: I have a seven piece group called The Dukes... front line of like Haywood and me

about_quotes.speaker: Charlie Holmes
about_quotes.evidence: The only name band I'd known him to be in was Erskine Hawkins... Because the personnel of the sax sections of Erskine Hawkins has... Haywood Henry. [INTERVIEWEE] Yeah, I remember that.
